outline: H1: How to Make Your Resume 100% ATS Friendly in 2026

faqs.question: How to make a resume ATS friendly in 2026?
faqs.answer: To make it ATS friendly, stick to standard fonts, single-column layouts, clear headings, exact keyword matches, and save as a PDF.

faqs.question: How do I know what keywords to include?
faqs.answer: Read the job description thoroughly. Look for required skills, tools, and certifications, and include those exact words in your resume.

faqs.question: Can I use columns in an ATS resume?
faqs.answer: It is highly recommended to avoid columns. Single-column layouts ensure that older and newer ATS parsers read your content chronologically.

faqs.question: Are italics and bold text okay?
faqs.answer: Yes, basic formatting like bold and italics are perfectly fine and help human recruiters scan your resume quickly.

faqs.question: How can I check if my resume is readable?
faqs.answer: Copy all text from your PDF and paste it into Notepad. If the text appears out of order or is missing words, your resume is not ATS friendly.

faqs.question: Does file size matter for ATS?
faqs.answer: Yes, keep the file size reasonable (under 1-2MB). Massive files usually indicate unnecessary embedded graphics.

faqs.question: Should I write out acronyms?
faqs.answer: Yes, use both the spelled-out version and the acronym, e.g., 'Master of Business Administration (MBA)'.

faqs.question: Where can I build an ATS friendly resume quickly?
faqs.answer: Use NextCV's specialized ATS builder to generate a perfectly formatted resume without any hassle.

title: Career Objective for Resume for Freshers
secondaryKeywords: give a career objective for resume
give me career objective for resume
seoTitle: Best Career Objectives for Resume for Freshers (Examples) | NextCV
metaDescription: Looking for the perfect career objective for a resume for freshers? Get top examples for IT, engineering, and management students to land your first job.
outline: H1: Crafting the Perfect Career Objective for a Fresher Resume
H2: Is an Objective Statement Still Relevant in 2026?
H2: How to Write an Impactful Career Objective
H3: Focus on the Company's Needs
H3: Mention Specific Skills
H3: Keep It Under 3 Sentences
H2: Top Examples of Career Objectives
H3: IT / Software Engineering Freshers
H3: BCA / MCA Freshers
H3: Non-IT / Business Management Freshers

faqs.question: What is a career objective in a resume?
faqs.answer: It is a short 2-3 sentence paragraph at the top of your resume explaining your skills, your career goals, and the value you can bring to the company.

faqs.question: Should freshers include a career objective?
faqs.answer: Yes, freshers should use an objective to clearly state their direction and highlight their strongest academic or technical skills.

faqs.question: What is a good career objective for a software engineer fresher?
faqs.answer: E.g., 'Highly motivated Computer Science graduate with strong skills in Java and Python. Seeking to leverage analytical abilities as a software engineer at a forward-thinking tech company.'

faqs.question: How long should a career objective be?
faqs.answer: It should be concise, ideally between 2 to 3 sentences. Avoid long, rambling paragraphs.

faqs.question: Should I customize my objective for every job?
faqs.answer: Yes! A generic objective looks lazy. Mention the specific role you are applying for and align your skills with their requirements.

faqs.question: Is a 'Summary' different from an 'Objective'?
faqs.answer: Yes, a summary highlights past experience, while an objective focuses on future goals and potential. Freshers usually use objectives.

faqs.question: What are common mistakes in a career objective?
faqs.answer: Being too self-focused ('I want a job that pays well') instead of company-focused ('I want to contribute to the team's success').

faqs.question: How can I automatically generate an objective?
faqs.answer: NextCV's AI tools can help generate the perfect, keyword-optimized career objective based on your target role.

secondaryKeywords: is cv and resume same in india

seoTitle: Resume vs CV in India: What's the Difference in 2026? | NextCV
metaDescription: What is the difference between a CV and a Resume in India? Discover which document you should submit for Indian corporate job applications in 2026.
outline: H1: Resume vs CV in India: Understanding the Key Differences
H2: Are CV and Resume Used Interchangeably in India?
H2: The Definition of a Resume
H3: Length, Focus, and Purpose
H2: The Definition of a CV (Curriculum Vitae)
H3: Detailed Academic and Research Focus

H3: Corporate Roles (IT, MNCs, Startups)
H2: When to Use a CV in India
H3: Academia, Fellowships, and Research
H2: Frequently Asked Questions

faqs.question: Is a CV and resume the same in India?
faqs.answer: In casual conversation, yes. However, technically a resume is a brief 1-2 page summary of skills, while a CV is a detailed, multi-page academic and professional record.

faqs.question: Should freshers use a CV or a resume?
faqs.answer: Freshers applying for corporate jobs (like IT, Finance, or Marketing) should strictly use a 1-page resume.

faqs.question: When should I submit a CV?
faqs.answer: Submit a CV when applying for academic positions, scientific research, fellowships, or medical roles where detailed publication history is required.

faqs.question: What does CV stand for?
faqs.answer: CV stands for 'Curriculum Vitae', which is Latin for 'course of life'.

faqs.question: Why do Indian recruiters ask for a CV when they mean a resume?
faqs.answer: It is a cultural quirk. In India, 'Send me your CV' usually just means 'Send me your resume'. Always send a 1-2 page document unless instructed otherwise.

faqs.question: Does NextCV build resumes or CVs?
faqs.answer: NextCV primarily builds highly optimized, concise resumes perfect for the Indian corporate and IT sectors.

faqs.question: Are ATS systems better at reading resumes or CVs?
faqs.answer: ATS systems can read both, but they are configured to look for specific keywords and concise skills commonly found in standard resumes.

faqs.question: What is a Biodata?
faqs.answer: A Biodata is an outdated format focusing heavily on personal details (religion, marital status, physical traits) and should not be used for modern corporate jobs.

faqs.question: What is the best resume format for non-IT freshers?
faqs.answer: A hybrid format works best, emphasizing core business skills (like Excel or Tally), college leadership roles, internships, and communication skills.

faqs.question: What skills should a BCom fresher include?
faqs.answer: Include skills like Advanced Excel, Financial Accounting, Tally ERP, Data Analysis, and strong verbal/written communication.

faqs.question: How do I make my non-IT resume stand out?
faqs.answer: Highlight specific achievements from college fests, leadership positions in student societies, or practical outcomes from business internships.

faqs.question: Do I need to worry about ATS for non-IT jobs?
faqs.answer: Yes, large banks, financial institutions, and FMCG companies all use Applicant Tracking Systems. ATS compliance is critical.

faqs.question: Should a BBA fresher include a career objective?
faqs.answer: Yes, a clear objective showing your passion for marketing, finance, or HR helps align your resume with the specific role.

faqs.question: Can I include volunteer work?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Volunteer work demonstrates initiative, teamwork, and soft skills which are highly valued in the non-IT corporate sector.

faqs.question: What software tools should non-IT freshers know?
faqs.answer: Proficiency in MS Office (especially Excel), Google Workspace, basic CRM tools, and Canva/Photoshop (for marketing) are huge pluses.

faqs.question: Where can I find non-IT resume templates?
faqs.answer: NextCV provides versatile, ATS-friendly templates that are perfect for finance, marketing, HR, and sales freshers.

faqs.question: What is the best resume format for an MNC company?
faqs.answer: A clean, ATS-compliant, single-page reverse-chronological layout is the gold standard for applying to multinational companies.

faqs.question: Do MNCs use Applicant Tracking Systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, almost 100% of large multinational companies use advanced ATS software like Workday or Taleo to screen thousands of resumes.

faqs.question: What do MNCs look for in freshers?
faqs.answer: They look for strong academic foundations, adaptability, excellent communication skills, technical certifications, and a problem-solving mindset.

faqs.question: Should I use a creative resume for an MNC?
faqs.answer: No. Unless you are applying for a design or creative agency role, a highly graphic resume will fail the MNC's ATS screening.

faqs.question: How important is grammar for MNC applications?
faqs.answer: It is critical. MNCs expect professional communication, and even minor typos can lead to instant rejection.

faqs.question: Should I include international exposure or language skills?
faqs.answer: Yes, fluency in foreign languages (like German or French) or international exchange programs are massive advantages for MNCs.

faqs.question: Do MNCs care about the college I attended?
faqs.answer: While top-tier colleges have an advantage, MNCs increasingly focus on your demonstrable skills, projects, and interview performance.

faqs.question: How can NextCV help me get into an MNC?
faqs.answer: NextCV's templates are modeled directly after the formatting requirements preferred by Fortune 500 companies and top MNCs.

faqs.question: What is the best resume format for IT jobs in India?
faqs.answer: A format that places your Technical Skills section immediately below your objective, followed by Projects and Education, is best for IT jobs.

faqs.question: How should I list my programming languages?
faqs.answer: Categorize them logically: 'Languages' (Java, Python), 'Frontend' (React, HTML), 'Backend' (Node.js), and 'Databases' (SQL, MongoDB).

faqs.question: Should I include my GitHub link?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. A well-maintained GitHub profile is often more valuable to a technical recruiter than your degree.

faqs.question: What keywords are essential for an IT resume?
faqs.answer: Include keywords like Agile, SDLC, RESTful APIs, Git, Cloud Computing (AWS/Azure), and specific programming languages relevant to the job.

faqs.question: How do I describe projects on an IT resume?
faqs.answer: State the project goal, the specific tech stack you used, your individual contribution, and the measurable outcome or performance improvement.

faqs.question: Are coding bootcamp certificates worth including?
faqs.answer: Yes, certifications from recognized bootcamps or platforms like Coursera/Udemy show continuous learning and passion for technology.

faqs.question: Do I need an ATS friendly resume for startups?
faqs.answer: While some startups review resumes manually, many scaling tech startups in India have adopted ATS tools. It's safest to be ATS-compliant.

faqs.question: Where can I find tech-specific ATS templates?
faqs.answer: NextCV offers dedicated technical resume templates built to showcase dense IT skills while remaining 100% ATS readable.

faqs.question: What is AI resume screening?
faqs.answer: AI screening uses advanced machine learning to analyze resumes not just for exact keywords, but for context, experience levels, and skill relationships.

faqs.question: How do I format my CV for AI screening?
faqs.answer: Use a highly structured, text-only PDF with standard semantic headings (Experience, Education, Skills) and avoid complex visual layouts.

faqs.question: Can AI read tables and columns?
faqs.answer: AI parsers struggle significantly with tables and multi-column formats. Always use a single-column, linear layout.

faqs.question: Does AI understand synonyms?
faqs.answer: Modern AI (like NLP models) can understand synonyms, but it is still highly recommended to use the exact phrasing found in the job description.

faqs.question: What is an AI readable CV template?
faqs.answer: It is a template stripped of unnecessary graphics, formatted in a strict top-down structure, ensuring no data is lost during extraction.

faqs.question: Should I use AI to write my resume?
faqs.answer: You can use AI tools to refine bullet points and objectives, but ensure the final content sounds natural and accurately reflects your true experience.

faqs.question: Why did the AI reject my resume?
faqs.answer: Likely due to unreadable formatting, missing critical skills, or a lack of quantifiable achievements demonstrating your impact.

faqs.question: Does NextCV optimize for AI bots?
faqs.answer: Yes, NextCV's templates are built precisely to be ingested seamlessly by both traditional ATS and modern AI screening tools.

faqs.question: What is the latest resume format for 2026?
faqs.answer: The latest trend is a hyper-minimalist, single-column, ATS-optimized hybrid format that heavily relies on metrics and data-driven achievements.

faqs.question: Are 2-page resumes acceptable in 2026?
faqs.answer: For freshers, 1 page remains the strict standard. For mid-level professionals, 2 pages are acceptable if the experience warrants it.

faqs.question: What should I remove from my 2026 resume?
faqs.answer: Remove objective statements that lack value, 'references available upon request', and outdated personal details like full home addresses.

faqs.question: How important are soft skills in 2026?
faqs.answer: Extremely important, especially adaptability and emotional intelligence, but they must be demonstrated through project examples, not just listed.

faqs.question: Is the 'Biodata' still used in 2026?
faqs.answer: No, the traditional biodata format is entirely obsolete in the modern corporate and IT job sectors in India.

faqs.question: How can I update my old resume to the 2026 format?
faqs.answer: Strip away graphics, reorganize your skills to the top, quantify your past projects, and use an ATS-friendly builder like NextCV.

faqs.question: What fonts are trending for resumes in 2026?
faqs.answer: Clean, sans-serif fonts like Roboto, Inter, and standard choices like Calibri remain the best options for readability and parsing.

faqs.question: Where can I download the latest 2026 resume template?
faqs.answer: NextCV offers a library of frequently updated templates that meet all current 2026 global and Indian recruiting standards.

faqs.question: How much does a resume builder cost in India?
faqs.answer: Costs range from completely free, to ₹200-₹500 per month for subscription apps, up to ₹2000+ for professional writing services.

faqs.question: Resume banane me kitna paisa lagta hai?
faqs.answer: Using an AI builder like NextCV is free. Local cyber cafes might charge ₹50-₹100 for basic typing, but those often fail ATS checks.

faqs.question: Why do some resume builders charge a subscription?
faqs.answer: Many builders lure users in for free and then demand a monthly subscription fee (like $3-$5) just to download the final PDF.

faqs.question: Is it worth paying a professional resume writer?
faqs.answer: For freshers, usually no. A good ATS builder is sufficient. Professionals with 10+ years of experience might benefit from specialized writers.

faqs.question: Are free resumes ATS friendly?
faqs.answer: Not all of them. Many free graphic tools produce PDFs that ATS cannot read. You must use a dedicated ATS resume builder like NextCV.

faqs.question: Does NextCV charge to download my resume?
faqs.answer: NextCV offers highly transparent pricing. Many core fresher templates and the ATS checker are entirely free to use and download.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest way to make a professional resume?
faqs.answer: The cheapest and most effective way is using a free, verified ATS-friendly template from a trusted platform and filling it out yourself.

faqs.question: Can I edit my resume later without paying again?
faqs.answer: On platforms like NextCV, you can create an account, save your progress, and edit or update your resume anytime at no extra cost.
